# Security Headers

HTTP security headers for restaurants.hultberg.org, set via `public/_headers`.

## How it works

Cloudflare Workers Static Assets honours a `_headers` file in the asset
directory (same syntax as Cloudflare Pages). The file lives in `public/`, which
Vite copies verbatim into the build output (`dist/client/_headers`) on
`npm run build`. The asset layer then applies the rules to every static
response — including `index.html`, which is the page securityheaders.com scans.

**Limitation:** `_headers` does *not* apply to responses generated by Worker
code (the `/api/*` JSON responses). Those don't need these headers — they're not
HTML rendered in a browsing context. If that ever changes, set headers directly
in `src/worker.js`.

Verify locally with `npx wrangler dev --port 8080` (a plain `vite preview` will
*not* apply `_headers`), then `curl -I http://localhost:8080/`. Use port 8080
specifically: `wrangler dev` defaults to 8787, but the Mapbox token's URL
restrictions whitelist `localhost:8080`, so the map only renders there.

## The headers

| Header | Value | Why |
|--------|-------|-----|
| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` | Force HTTPS for a year. No `preload` token — that's a hard-to-reverse commitment we don't need. |
| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Stop MIME-type sniffing. |
| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` | Block framing (clickjacking). Redundant with CSP `frame-ancestors` but securityheaders.com checks for it explicitly. |
| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Don't leak full URLs to third parties. |
| `Permissions-Policy` | `geolocation=(self), camera=(), …` | Disable unused browser features. **`geolocation=(self)` is required** for the "Near Me" GPS feature. |
| `Content-Security-Policy` | see below | Restrict resource origins. |

## CSP directives — why each origin is allowed

The CSP is scoped to what the **production browser** actually loads:

- `script-src … static.cloudflareinsights.com` — Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon (in `index.html`).
- `style-src 'unsafe-inline' … fonts.googleapis.com` — Google Fonts CSS + inline styles injected by React/Tailwind/Mapbox.
- `font-src … fonts.gstatic.com` — Google Font files (Space Grotesk, JetBrains Mono).
- `img-src 'self' data: blob: https:` — restaurant/Google Places photos (arbitrary https hosts), Mapbox marker images (data:/blob:).
- `connect-src 'self' *.mapbox.com static.cloudflareinsights.com cloudflareinsights.com` — same-origin `/api/*` proxy calls, Mapbox style/tiles/telemetry, analytics beacon reporting.
- `worker-src / child-src 'self' blob:` — Mapbox GL JS runs its renderer in a blob: web worker.
- `frame-ancestors 'none'`, `base-uri 'self'`, `form-action 'self'`, `object-src 'none'` — lock down framing, base tag, form targets, plugins.

`'unsafe-inline'` is present for scripts/styles. securityheaders.com grades CSP on
*presence*, not strictness, so this still scores A+ while avoiding a nonce
pipeline that would fight Mapbox's inline injection.

## What goes through the Worker proxy (NOT in connect-src)

These are called **server-side** via `/api/*` and never directly from the
browser in production, so they don't need CSP entries:

- `api.anthropic.com` (Claude) → `/api/claude`, `/api/extract-restaurant`
- `maps.googleapis.com` (geocoding/places) → `/api/google-maps`
  (the direct `maps.googleapis.com` call in `locationService.ts` is a
  dev-only path)
- CORS proxies (`codetabs`, `allorigins`, etc.) in the extractor services are
  legacy/server code, not the prod browser path.

## Adding a new external origin

When the frontend starts loading from a new host (a new font CDN, image host,
API called directly from the browser), add it to the matching directive in
`public/_headers`, rebuild, and re-test the map + console with `wrangler dev`.
A missing `connect-src` entry fails **silently** — the fetch is blocked with
only a console error, no user-facing message.
